site.name: Talha Bilal
site.headline: Production PKI, digital signing & IAM platforms in Java
site.role: Senior Software Engineer & Technical Team Lead
site.focus: PKI · Digital Signing · Keycloak · Java Card · HSM

summary: Senior engineer and technical lead with 5+ years shipping secure, enterprise-grade systems in Java Spring Boot, PKI, and identity. Specializes in certificate lifecycle, high-assurance digital signing, HSM integration (AWS CloudHSM, Utimaco, SoftHSM2), end-to-end Keycloak with custom SPIs, and Java Card applets — from regulated signing workflows to centralized IAM.

paragraphs: I help organizations run trust infrastructure in production: certificate authorities, OCSP/CRL, S/MIME and PDF signing, timestamp authorities, and HSM-backed crypto services.
As Technical Team Lead at Dictalabs, I combine architecture, hands-on Java development, and code review — working with product and security stakeholders in audit-sensitive environments.
I collaborate effectively with distributed teams: clear written specs, async communication, and overlap with European and Middle Eastern business hours.

items.label: Time zone
items.detail: Based in UTC+5 with 4–6 hours overlap with CET/GMT and flexible sync for US East

items.label: Communication
items.detail: Professional English — documentation, design notes, incident reports, and stakeholder calls

items.label: Delivery style
items.detail: Remote-friendly rituals: written RFCs, PR-based review, CI-gated releases, and on-call runbooks

items.label: Engagement
items.detail: Open to employer-of-record, direct contract, or full-time remote with transparent availability

skillGroups.label: Security, PKI & IAM
items: Keycloak SPI (Authenticator, User Storage, Protocol Mapper)

experience.industry: Enterprise digital trust & signing (B2B PKI platform)
experience.title: Technical Team Lead — Java Spring Boot & PKI
bullets: Lead engineers owning microservices for PKI, CMS/PKCS#7 signing, and HSM-backed key operations across production tenants.
Architected PKCS#11 integration layer (AWS CloudHSM, SoftHSM2) — improved signing stability via pooled sessions, idempotent retries, and operational runbooks.
Delivered end-to-end Keycloak: multi-realm OIDC, client policies, and custom SPIs (Authenticator, User Storage, Protocol Mapper) for certificate-aware login.
Ship Java Card 3.x applets (RSA/ECDSA on-card) with GlobalPlatform lifecycle and host issuance tooling.
Drive RFC 3161 timestamping, OCSP/CRL hardening, and incident response with security and product leadership.

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer — Java Spring Boot & PKI
bullets: Built production CA stack: issuance, renewal, OCSP/CRL responders, and tenant-scoped certificate policies.
Developed S/MIME/CMS email signing and Crypto-Core services integrated with enterprise HSMs and audit trails.
Implemented Java Card applets and PC/SC host applications for smart-card issuance and secure key ceremonies.
Established Docker-based CI/CD, API regression suites, and performance tuning for high-volume signing paths.
Delivered multi-tenant PDF/signing workflows with approval chains and compliance-oriented logging.

caseStudies.title: Keycloak IAM with PKI-backed authentication
caseStudies.problem: Platform needed centralized SSO/OIDC while honoring existing certificate-based credentials and tenant-specific login policies.
caseStudies.role: Technical lead — architecture, custom SPI development, and Spring Boot service integration.
caseStudies.approach: Designed realms and clients per tenant; implemented Authenticator and User Storage SPIs to map X.509/certificate identities to Keycloak sessions; extended Protocol Mapper for claims required by signing microservices.
caseStudies.outcome: Single IAM layer for admin and customer apps, reduced bespoke auth code in services, and auditable login flows aligned with PKI issuance.

caseStudies.title: HSM signing platform at scale
caseStudies.problem: Signing microservices faced intermittent PKCS#11 failures and latency spikes under concurrent enterprise workloads.
caseStudies.role: Senior/lead engineer — PKCS#11 service design and production hardening.
caseStudies.approach: Introduced REST PKCS#11 abstraction with session pooling, structured error taxonomy, Redis-backed coordination where needed, and metrics-friendly logging for on-call.
caseStudies.outcome: More predictable signing paths for PDF, email, and timestamp services; faster incident diagnosis and fewer customer-impacting outages.
